caption.text:
The greatest library in ancient India. One fire took almost everything.

Nalanda's library held manuscripts collected over 700 years of scholarship — Buddhist texts, medical treatises, astronomical calculations, works on logic, grammar, and philosophy. In 1200 CE, troops under Bakhtiyar Khalji sacked the monastery and set it ablaze. Tibetan pilgrim records document monks fleeing with whatever texts they could carry, while others were killed where they stood.

Archaeological excavations in the 1920s found a thick layer of ashes across the entire site — physical proof of the fire. The vast manuscript libraries of Magadha were mostly lost. Today, a few surviving fragments sit in the Nalanda Archaeological Museum and in scattered Tibetan monasteries. The rest is smoke and memory.

We know the library existed. We know what was lost. We will never know the full scope of what burned.

If you could recover one text, what subject would you choose?

text.line_1: Monks fled with what they could carry.

text.line_2: What would you try to save?
